mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates. This dual signal of sustained monetary tightness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=3.64% and CPIAUCSL=330.293 near restrictive threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window during periods when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor market momentum that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without requiring a specific trigger threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month change and 20-day SPX returns remains above 0.08 in absolute value, or monthly payroll declines do not precede negative SPX returns by 10-30 trading days in at least 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1400 thousand units annualized, it signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction. This demand shock propagates to equity valuations over a 60-day horizon as consensus earnings forecasts are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST=1487.0 thousand units, above trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > -1%) when HOUST falls below 1400, or the correlation between HOUST and subsequent 60-day returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling a supply-side contraction coupled with labor market slack. This stagflationary signal produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal (both conditions met) shows Rank IC below 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) exhibit a 3-month declining trend, indicating consumer demand weakness that foreshadows lower corporate revenues and equity valuations. This demand deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day horizon as market participants rapidly reprrice earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following RSAFS declines are not significantly negative (mean > 0.1%), or Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day returns falls below 0.06 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.0%, signaling tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressure and corporate labor cost inflation. This cost shock reduces profit margins and produces negative equity returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings sustainability.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage acceleration risk
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > 0%) when UNRATE < 4.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE level and subsequent 60-day returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 3.5%, creating a regime where monetary policy is restrictive relative to growth fundamentals. This policy-growth mismatch produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days as recession risk repricing accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1=24055.749 billion chained 2017 dollars and FEDFUNDS=3.64%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC of the joint signal falls below 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits sustained month-over-month growth above 0.8% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable, signaling healthy supply-side expansion without inflationary pressure. This benign supply expansion produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as profit margins expand and growth expectations strengthen.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.8% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.3%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC of the joint signal falls below 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window.
